properties.text: Block RLP size must not exceed MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE (EIP-7934). The state_transition function must reject any block where len(rlp.encode(block)) > MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE before any further processing occurs.

reachability.notes: A P2P peer can send arbitrarily large block payloads. If the RLP size check is bypassed or occurs after resource-intensive decoding, oversized blocks cause memory exhaustion. This is a Fusaka-specific EIP-7934 defense.

properties.severity_justification: Bypass allows resource exhaustion on all receiving nodes, causing network-wide DoS. The check must execute before any block processing to be effective.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a block exceeding MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE that bypasses the size check or causes resource exhaustion before the check triggers.

properties.text: Ommers tuple must be empty in post-merge Ethereum. For all valid blocks: block.ommers == (). Any block with non-empty ommers must be rejected by state_transition.

reachability.notes: A malicious proposer can set ommers to non-empty in a proposed block. Both state_transition (SG-003) and validate_header (SG-005) enforce this invariant, providing defense-in-depth.

properties.severity_justification: Non-empty ommers could trigger pre-merge code paths in client implementations, potentially causing consensus divergence between clients.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Must demonstrate that a block with non-empty ommers is accepted by at least one client implementation.

properties.text: All 8 computed roots/hashes must match header values exactly after block execution. For all valid blocks: gas_used, transactions_root, state_root, receipt_root, bloom, withdrawals_root, blob_gas_used, and requests_hash must each match the corresponding header field.

reachability.notes: An attacker proposes a block with inconsistent header fields. If any of the 8 comparison checks can be bypassed, the attacker could commit an invalid state transition causing a consensus split across the network.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing any root/hash verification allows invalid state transitions to be committed, leading to consensus failure and potential fund loss across the entire network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a block with mismatched computed vs header values that is accepted as valid.

properties.text: Chain must retain at most 255 blocks after appending a new block. After state_transition succeeds: len(chain.blocks) <= 255.

reachability.notes: Pruning is an internal operation triggered automatically. Failure would only cause memory growth over time but is not directly exploitable by an external attacker.

properties.severity_justification: Failure causes unbounded memory growth but does not directly compromise consensus or security.

properties.text: Transaction sender identity cannot be spoofed. The recover_sender function must correctly derive the canonical sender address from the ECDSA signature (v, r, s), rejecting malleable signatures (enforcing low-s per EIP-2).

reachability.notes: An attacker crafts a transaction with a forged or malleable signature. If recover_sender has an edge-case bug, the attacker could impersonate another account and spend its funds.

properties.severity_justification: Signature spoofing allows an attacker to impersonate any account, transfer its funds, and increment its nonce -- complete account takeover.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a crafted signature that recover_sender resolves to a different account than the actual signer.

properties.text: RLP encoding and decoding must be deterministic and collision-free. For all blocks B1 != B2: rlp.encode(B1) != rlp.encode(B2). Non-deterministic encoding would cause consensus splits between clients.

reachability.notes: Multiple subgraphs rely on RLP for serialization. If an attacker can craft a payload that encodes differently across implementations, clients would compute different block hashes and diverge.

properties.severity_justification: Non-deterministic RLP encoding causes consensus splits -- different clients accept different blocks, fragmenting the network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a payload where two compliant implementations produce different RLP encodings.

properties.text: Keccak256 hash function must be collision-resistant. Block hash computation, parent hash verification, and state/receipt/transaction root computations all depend on keccak256. A hash collision would allow block forgery.

reachability.notes: Keccak256 provides 256-bit security. A collision is computationally infeasible with current technology, but an implementation bug in hash computation or comparison could be exploitable.

properties.severity_justification: A hash collision or comparison bug allows block forgery and chain reorganization, undermining all cryptographic integrity guarantees.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate either a keccak256 collision or an implementation bug in hash comparison that leads to accepting a forged block.

properties.text: Consensus layer withdrawal data must be processed faithfully. The process_withdrawals function (SG-014) must increase account balances by exactly withdrawal.amount * 10^9 Wei for each withdrawal, and never decrease any balance.

reachability.notes: Withdrawals cross from CL to EL via Engine API. The CL is semi-trusted. The EL does not independently validate withdrawal validity -- it trusts the CL. However, an incorrect Gwei-to-Wei scaling factor in the EL implementation could inflate/deflate withdrawal amounts.

properties.severity_justification: An incorrect scaling factor (10^9) would create or destroy ETH across the entire network, affecting total supply integrity.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate that a withdrawal processes with an incorrect amount due to scaling error in the implementation.

properties.text: Withdrawal processing must only increase balances, never decrease them. For all withdrawals w and all accounts a: balance_after(a) >= balance_before(a).

reachability.notes: If a bug causes withdrawals to subtract instead of add, or if integer overflow causes a wraparound, balances could decrease.

properties.severity_justification: Balance decrease during withdrawal processing constitutes fund loss for validators, a critical economic failure.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Must demonstrate withdrawal processing that decreases an account balance.

properties.text: System contracts must contain code before checked system transactions are executed. process_checked_system_transaction (SG-009) must verify len(system_contract_code) > 0 and raise InvalidBlock if the contract has no code.

reachability.notes: System contracts are deployed at fork activation. If a fork transition fails to deploy them, checked system transactions would call empty addresses. The code check in SG-009 catches this, but unchecked variants (SG-010) do not.

properties.severity_justification: Missing system contract code would cause block processing to fail, but the checked variant catches this. However, the unchecked variant for beacon roots/history storage does not check, potentially allowing silent failures.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Must demonstrate a scenario where a system contract lacks code after fork activation.

properties.text: Checked system transactions must succeed without error. process_checked_system_transaction (SG-009) must raise InvalidBlock if system_tx_output.error is not None.

reachability.notes: If an attacker can cause a system contract (WITHDRAWAL_REQUEST_ADDRESS or CONSOLIDATION_REQUEST_ADDRESS) to revert, the entire block becomes invalid. If the error check is missing or bypassed, invalid return data could be committed.

properties.severity_justification: If a system contract can be made to revert on every block, no valid blocks can be produced, halting the entire chain.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a mechanism to consistently cause system contract reversion, blocking all block production.

properties.text: Unchecked system transactions do not validate contract code existence or execution success. process_unchecked_system_transaction (SG-010) must be used only for beacon roots and history storage system contracts, whose failure is non-critical.

reachability.notes: Unchecked system transactions for beacon roots and history storage are assumed safe because their failure only affects ring buffer data, not consensus validity. A compromised CL could inject incorrect beacon roots without detection.

properties.severity_justification: Corruption of beacon roots ring buffer affects smart contracts relying on EIP-4788, but does not directly break consensus.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Must demonstrate beacon root corruption leading to incorrect smart contract behavior.

properties.text: Base fee increase must be at least 1 when gas used exceeds target. For calculate_base_fee_per_gas: when parent_gas_used > parent_gas_target, base_fee_per_gas_delta >= 1.

reachability.notes: A proposer controls gas usage within their block. If the minimum increase of 1 can be circumvented, the base fee could stagnate at 0, allowing free transactions.

properties.severity_justification: The asymmetry is by design (EIP-1559). Exploitation requires sustained block proposer control and only affects fee economics, not consensus safety.

properties.text: Transaction validation checks must be ordered from general to specific. The transaction_validation_dispatch (SG-004 of exceptions.py) must check type first, then fees, then gas limits, then blob-specific checks, then authorization checks.

reachability.notes: An attacker submits transactions designed to maximize validation cost. If cheap general checks are moved after expensive specific checks, the attacker can waste validator CPU. However, this is a low-effort single-peer DoS which may be out-of-scope.

properties.severity_justification: Reordering validation checks wastes CPU on invalid transactions but does not cause consensus issues. Out-of-scope per high-effort DoS exclusion.

properties.text: All blob versioned hashes must have version prefix VERSIONED_HASH_VERSION_KZG (0x01). For all blob transactions: forall hash in tx.blob_versioned_hashes: hash[0] == 0x01.

reachability.notes: An attacker submits a blob transaction with versioned hashes that have the correct 0x01 prefix but correspond to invalid KZG commitments. The EL only checks the version byte; full KZG proof verification is performed by the CL.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting a non-KZG versioned hash would allow uncommitted blob data to pass EL validation, breaking data availability guarantees.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a blob transaction with non-0x01 versioned hash prefix that passes EL validation.

properties.text: Gas refund must be capped at gas_used // 5 (EIP-3529). In process_transaction (SG-013): tx_gas_refund = min(gas_used // 5, refund_counter). The refund must never exceed 20% of gas consumed.

reachability.notes: An attacker crafts a transaction with operations that generate large refund counters (e.g., SSTORE clearing). If the cap calculation is incorrect, excess refunds allow the attacker to receive more gas back than expected.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect refund cap allows economic exploitation: transactions cost less than intended, enabling spam or resource exhaustion at reduced cost.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a transaction receiving more than gas_used // 5 in refunds.

properties.text: EIP-7623 calldata floor gas cost must be enforced after refund. In process_transaction (SG-013): tx_gas_used = max(gas_used - refund, calldata_floor_gas_cost). The final gas used must be at least the calldata floor.

reachability.notes: An attacker crafts a transaction with large calldata but operations that generate refunds to pay below the floor. The max() ensures the minimum charge is met regardless of refunds.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing the calldata floor allows cheap data availability exploitation, undercutting the economic model designed to prevent state bloat.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a transaction paying less than calldata_floor_gas_cost after refunds.

properties.text: Post-merge header fields must be zero/empty. validate_header (SG-005) must enforce: difficulty == 0, nonce == 0x0000000000000000, and ommers_hash == EMPTY_OMMER_HASH.

reachability.notes: A malicious proposer sets post-merge fields to non-zero values to trigger pre-merge code paths. Three separate invariants (INV-005, INV-006, INV-007) enforce these constraints.

properties.severity_justification: Could trigger pre-merge code paths causing client-specific divergence, but defense-in-depth (double enforcement in state_transition and validate_header) reduces risk.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Must demonstrate a client accepting a block with non-zero difficulty, non-zero nonce, or non-empty ommers hash.

properties.text: Sender accounts with code must have valid delegation designations to send transactions. check_transaction (SG-006) must reject transactions from accounts with code unless the code is a valid delegation designation (EIP-7702).

reachability.notes: An attacker exploits EIP-7702 authorization list validation to gain code execution authority over accounts they do not control. If the delegation check is bypassed, contracts could initiate unauthorized transactions.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing the delegation check allows unauthorized code execution authority over victim accounts, potentially draining funds.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a SetCode transaction that gains execution authority over an account without valid authorization.

properties.text: Engine API must not be exposed to untrusted networks. Engine API communication between CL and EL must be JWT-authenticated and restricted to localhost or trusted networks.

reachability.notes: This is a configuration/infrastructure concern. Vulnerabilities requiring exposed APIs are explicitly out-of-scope per the bug bounty program.

properties.severity_justification: An exposed Engine API allows injection of malicious block parameters and withdrawals, but this is an operator configuration issue.

properties.text: Transaction gas must not exceed remaining block gas. check_transaction (SG-006) must enforce: tx.gas <= (block_gas_limit - block_gas_used).

reachability.notes: An attacker submits a transaction with gas exceeding the remaining block capacity. The check prevents block gas limit overrun.

properties.severity_justification: If a transaction bypasses the gas limit check, block gas accounting becomes inconsistent, leading to consensus failure.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a transaction accepted with gas exceeding block gas availability.

properties.text: Transaction blob gas must not exceed remaining block blob gas. check_transaction (SG-006) must enforce: calculate_total_blob_gas(tx) <= blob_gas_available.

reachability.notes: An attacker submits blob transactions to exceed the block blob gas limit. If the calculation has edge cases (e.g., overflow in total_blob_gas), the limit could be bypassed.

properties.severity_justification: Blob gas limit bypass would allow excess blob data in a block, breaking data availability assumptions and potentially causing consensus issues.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a block accepted with total blob gas exceeding the block limit.

properties.text: For EIP-1559 transactions: max_fee_per_gas >= max_priority_fee_per_gas. check_transaction (SG-006) must reject transactions where the priority fee exceeds the max fee.

reachability.notes: An attacker submits an EIP-1559 transaction with max_priority_fee_per_gas > max_fee_per_gas. If accepted, the effective gas price calculation could underflow or produce unexpected results.

properties.severity_justification: Priority fee > max fee creates inconsistent fee calculations. In fixed-width integer implementations, this could cause underflow.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate acceptance of a transaction where priority fee exceeds max fee, or demonstrate arithmetic issues from such values.

properties.text: For EIP-1559 transactions: max_fee_per_gas >= base_fee_per_gas. check_transaction (SG-006) must reject transactions where the max fee is insufficient to cover the current base fee.

reachability.notes: An attacker submits a transaction with max_fee_per_gas below the current base fee. If accepted, the transaction executes without paying sufficient base fee, undermining EIP-1559 economics.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting transactions below base fee undermines the fee market mechanism and allows under-priced execution.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a transaction accepted and executed with max_fee_per_gas < base_fee_per_gas.

properties.text: Blob transactions must have between 1 and BLOB_COUNT_LIMIT blobs. check_transaction (SG-006) must enforce: 0 < len(tx.blob_versioned_hashes) <= BLOB_COUNT_LIMIT.

reachability.notes: An attacker submits a blob transaction with zero blobs or exceeding MAX_BLOB_COUNT. The check prevents empty blob transactions and limits data availability overhead.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting a blob transaction with zero blobs wastes block capacity. Exceeding MAX_BLOB_COUNT breaks data availability assumptions.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a blob transaction accepted with 0 blobs or > MAX_BLOB_COUNT blobs.

properties.text: Blob and SetCode transactions must not create contracts. check_transaction (SG-006) must reject blob and SetCode transactions where tx.to is None (contract creation).

reachability.notes: An attacker submits a blob or SetCode transaction with to=None. If the contract creation check is bypassed, the transaction type semantics are violated.

properties.severity_justification: Allowing contract creation for blob/SetCode types violates EIP semantics and could introduce unexpected state changes.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a blob or SetCode transaction creating a contract.

properties.text: SetCode transactions must have non-empty authorization list. check_transaction (SG-006) must reject SetCode transactions with empty authorization lists.

reachability.notes: A SetCode transaction without authorizations has no purpose. Accepting it could indicate a validation bypass that might extend to malformed authorizations.

properties.severity_justification: Empty authorization lists are semantically invalid. Acceptance may indicate broader validation issues in EIP-7702 handling.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a SetCode transaction with empty authorization list being accepted.

properties.text: Sender nonce must match transaction nonce exactly. check_transaction (SG-006) must enforce: sender_account.nonce == tx.nonce. Both too-low and too-high nonces must be rejected.

reachability.notes: An attacker submits transactions with nonce mismatches. If nonce verification can be bypassed, replay attacks or nonce-gap exploitation become possible.

properties.severity_justification: Nonce mismatch acceptance enables transaction replay or ordering attacks, potentially leading to fund loss or state corruption.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a transaction accepted with nonce != sender account nonce.

properties.text: Sender balance must be sufficient for maximum possible cost. check_transaction (SG-006) must enforce: sender_balance >= (tx.gas * max_fee_per_gas) + tx.value + (blob_gas * max_fee_per_blob_gas for blob txs).

reachability.notes: An attacker with near-boundary balance values crafts transactions with extreme gas parameters. If the balance check overflows in fixed-width integer implementations, insufficient balances could pass.

properties.severity_justification: Balance check bypass allows spending without sufficient funds, creating ETH from nothing -- a fund loss vulnerability.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a transaction accepted with sender balance insufficient for max_gas_fee + value.

properties.text: Nonce must be incremented before message execution. process_transaction (SG-013) must call increment_nonce(state, sender) before process_message_call(message).

reachability.notes: If the nonce is not incremented before execution, reentrancy or cross-transaction nonce reuse could occur within the same block.

properties.severity_justification: Late nonce increment could enable reentrancy attacks where the same nonce is used for multiple transactions within a single block execution.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate nonce reuse within block execution due to ordering bug.

properties.text: Sender pays effective_gas_fee and blob_gas_fee upfront before execution. process_transaction (SG-013) must deduct max gas fees from sender balance before message execution.

reachability.notes: If fees are not deducted upfront, the sender could spend the balance during execution and avoid paying gas fees.

properties.severity_justification: Late fee deduction allows execution with insufficient funds, creating a fee-evasion exploit.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a transaction executing without upfront fee deduction.

properties.text: Unused gas must be refunded at effective_gas_price. process_transaction (SG-013) must refund gas_left * effective_gas_price to the sender after execution.

reachability.notes: If the refund uses an incorrect gas price (e.g., base fee instead of effective price, or a higher price), the sender receives incorrect refund amounts.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect refund amounts cause economic imbalance but do not directly compromise consensus. Over/under-refunding affects ETH supply accuracy.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a transaction where the refund amount does not equal gas_left * effective_gas_price.

properties.text: Miner/coinbase must receive only the priority fee portion. process_transaction (SG-013) must pay coinbase: tx_gas_used * (effective_gas_price - base_fee_per_gas). Base fee must be burned, not paid to the miner.

reachability.notes: If the coinbase receives the full effective gas price instead of just the priority fee, the base fee is not burned, undermining EIP-1559's deflationary mechanism.

properties.severity_justification: Paying base fee to coinbase instead of burning it breaks EIP-1559 economics and prevents proper ETH supply management.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate coinbase receiving more than tx_gas_used * priority_fee_per_gas.

properties.text: Accounts marked for deletion must be destroyed after transaction execution. process_transaction (SG-013) must destroy all accounts in accounts_to_delete set after message execution.

reachability.notes: SELFDESTRUCT marks accounts for deletion. If the deletion fails, state divergence occurs between spec and implementation.

properties.severity_justification: Failure to delete accounts causes state trie divergence, leading to consensus failure on state root comparison.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate accounts surviving despite being in accounts_to_delete, causing state root mismatch.

properties.text: Beacon roots system transaction must execute before any user transactions. apply_body (SG-011) must call process_unchecked_system_transaction(BEACON_ROOTS_ADDRESS) as the first operation.

reachability.notes: If beacon roots are written after user transactions, those transactions cannot access the expected beacon root data via the ring buffer. The ordering is protocol-mandated.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect ordering causes beacon root data to be unavailable during transaction execution, breaking EIP-4788 dependent contracts.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Must demonstrate beacon root unavailability during transaction execution due to ordering violation.

properties.text: History storage system transaction must execute after beacon roots and before user transactions. apply_body (SG-011) must call process_unchecked_system_transaction(HISTORY_STORAGE_ADDRESS) as the second operation.

reachability.notes: History storage must be updated before user transactions to ensure correct historical data access.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect ordering causes stale history data during transaction execution, affecting contracts querying historical block hashes.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Must demonstrate history storage data inconsistency due to ordering violation.

properties.text: Transactions must be processed in sequential order within a block. apply_body (SG-011) must process transactions in the order they appear in the block's transaction list.

reachability.notes: Out-of-order transaction processing would produce different state roots, receipts, and logs. The sequential ordering is a consensus invariant.

properties.severity_justification: Out-of-order processing produces different state roots across clients, causing consensus failure.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Must demonstrate that reordered transaction processing produces a different state root.

properties.text: Withdrawals must be processed after all transactions. apply_body (SG-011) must call process_withdrawals after all transactions have been processed.

reachability.notes: Processing withdrawals before all transactions would alter state during transaction execution, producing different state roots.

properties.severity_justification: Premature withdrawal processing alters balances visible to transactions, causing state root divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Must demonstrate state root difference when withdrawals are processed before all transactions.

properties.text: General purpose requests must be processed last in block body. apply_body (SG-011) must call process_general_purpose_requests after withdrawals and all transactions.

reachability.notes: Request processing depends on the final state after all transactions and withdrawals. Premature processing would miss or include incorrect requests.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect request ordering produces different requests_hash, causing consensus failure.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Must demonstrate requests_hash mismatch due to incorrect processing order.

properties.text: Request types must be appended in ascending order: deposits, then withdrawals, then consolidations. process_general_purpose_requests (SG-012) must assemble requests in this exact order.

reachability.notes: The request ordering determines the requests_hash. An attacker can craft transactions that generate deposit logs and trigger withdrawal/consolidation requests. If the ordering is violated, the requests_hash will differ between implementations.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect request ordering produces a different requests_hash, causing the block to be rejected or causing consensus splits.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a block where request ordering affects the requests_hash.

properties.text: Deposit requests must be parsed from block output logs, not from system transactions. process_general_purpose_requests (SG-012) must derive deposit requests from log entries via parse_deposit_requests.

reachability.notes: If the log parsing is incorrect (wrong contract address, wrong ABI decoding), fraudulent deposit requests could be injected or legitimate deposits could be missed.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect deposit parsing could inject fraudulent validator deposits or miss legitimate ones, affecting consensus layer validator set.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate fraudulent deposit requests injected via crafted log entries, or missing legitimate deposits.

properties.text: Withdrawal and consolidation requests must come from checked system transactions that must succeed. process_general_purpose_requests (SG-012) must call process_checked_system_transaction for withdrawal and consolidation request contracts.

reachability.notes: If the checked variant is replaced with unchecked, system contract failures would be silently ignored, producing incorrect request data. An attacker could manipulate system contract state to cause failures.

properties.severity_justification: If system contract calls fail silently, withdrawal and consolidation requests are lost or corrupted, affecting validator operations network-wide.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a system contract failure that is not caught, leading to incorrect request data in the block.

properties.text: Block number must be >= 1. validate_header (SG-005) must reject blocks with header.number < 1 (genesis block is 0).

reachability.notes: A peer sends a block with number 0. If accepted, it could corrupt chain state or trigger genesis-specific code paths.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting a block with number 0 could trigger unexpected code paths but is caught by the very first validation check.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a block with number < 1 being accepted by a client.

properties.text: Block timestamps must be strictly increasing. validate_header (SG-005) must enforce: header.timestamp > parent_header.timestamp.

reachability.notes: A proposer sets timestamp <= parent timestamp. If accepted, time-dependent operations (e.g., TIMESTAMP opcode, time-locked contracts) could behave incorrectly.

properties.severity_justification: Non-increasing timestamps break time-dependent protocol assumptions and smart contract behavior.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a block with non-increasing timestamp being accepted.

properties.text: Block numbers must be sequential. validate_header (SG-005) must enforce: header.number == parent_header.number + 1.

reachability.notes: A proposer sends a block with a gap or duplicate number. If accepted, chain continuity is broken.

properties.severity_justification: Non-sequential block numbers break chain continuity and could enable block number reuse attacks.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a block with non-sequential number being accepted.

properties.text: Extra data must be limited to 32 bytes. validate_header (SG-005) must enforce: len(header.extra_data) <= 32.

reachability.notes: A proposer includes excessive extra data. Unbounded extra data could be used for block size inflation.

properties.severity_justification: Excess extra data causes minor block inflation but is bounded by the RLP size limit.

properties.text: Parent hash must cryptographically match parent header. validate_header (SG-005) must enforce: header.parent_hash == keccak256(rlp.encode(parent_header)).

reachability.notes: An attacker creates a block with a parent_hash that does not match the actual parent header hash. If accepted, chain reorganization or fork attacks become possible.

properties.severity_justification: Parent hash mismatch enables chain reorganization attacks, undermining the fundamental chain integrity.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a block accepted with parent_hash != keccak256(rlp.encode(parent_header)).

properties.text: Excess blob gas must match calculation from parent. validate_header (SG-005) must enforce: header.excess_blob_gas == calculate_excess_blob_gas(parent_header).

reachability.notes: A proposer sets incorrect excess_blob_gas. If the calculation has an edge case (e.g., underflow when target_blob_gas > parent_blob_gas_used), clients may diverge.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect excess blob gas affects blob fee calculations, potentially causing consensus splits between clients with different arithmetic handling.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a block accepted with incorrect excess_blob_gas or demonstrate arithmetic edge case causing divergence.

properties.text: Gas used must not exceed gas limit. validate_header (SG-005) must enforce: header.gas_used <= header.gas_limit.

reachability.notes: A proposer sets gas_used > gas_limit in the header. If accepted, blocks can exceed their declared capacity.

properties.severity_justification: Gas used > gas limit breaks the gas accounting model and could cause consensus failure on the gas_used root check.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a block accepted with gas_used > gas_limit.

properties.text: Gas limit change must be bounded by parent_gas_limit // GAS_LIMIT_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR. check_gas_limit (SG-015) must enforce: abs(gas_limit - parent_gas_limit) < parent_gas_limit // 1024.

reachability.notes: A proposer adjusts gas limit beyond bounds. If the check fails, rapid gas limit changes could destabilize the network.

properties.severity_justification: Even if the check is bypassed for a single block, the impact is limited. Exploitation requires sustained proposer control.

properties.text: Gas limit must be >= GAS_LIMIT_MINIMUM (5000). check_gas_limit (SG-015) must enforce this absolute minimum.

reachability.notes: A proposer sets gas limit below 5000. If accepted, blocks cannot include even minimal transactions.

properties.severity_justification: Extremely low gas limit is disruptive but requires sustained proposer control to be effective.

properties.text: System transactions must always originate from SYSTEM_ADDRESS (0xff...fe). process_system_transaction (SG-008) must set origin and caller to SYSTEM_ADDRESS.

reachability.notes: If the system address is set incorrectly, system contracts receive calls from unexpected callers, potentially bypassing caller-based access controls.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect system address allows non-system callers to interact with system contracts as if privileged.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Must demonstrate a system transaction executing with a caller other than SYSTEM_ADDRESS.

properties.text: System transaction gas must be fixed at SYSTEM_TRANSACTION_GAS (30,000,000). process_system_transaction (SG-008) must use exactly this gas amount.

reachability.notes: If system transaction gas is not fixed, a lower value could cause system contracts to fail due to insufficient gas, while a higher value wastes resources.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect gas allocation could cause system contract failures (too low) or resource waste (too high).
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Must demonstrate system transaction executing with gas != 30,000,000.

properties.text: System transactions must not transfer value. process_system_transaction (SG-008) must set should_transfer_value=False and value=0.

reachability.notes: If value transfer is enabled for system transactions, ETH could be moved from/to the SYSTEM_ADDRESS, creating or destroying supply.

properties.severity_justification: Value transfer in system transactions could create or destroy ETH, affecting total supply integrity.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Must demonstrate a system transaction transferring non-zero value.

properties.text: Receipt succeeded field must be True if and only if error is None. make_receipt (SG-007) must set succeeded = (error is None).

reachability.notes: If the succeeded field does not match the error state, clients disagree on transaction success/failure. This affects receipt_root computation.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect receipt succeeded field causes receipt_root divergence, leading to consensus failure.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Must demonstrate a receipt where succeeded != (error is None).

properties.text: Receipt bloom must be computed from transaction logs via logs_bloom. make_receipt (SG-007) must set bloom = logs_bloom(logs).

reachability.notes: Incorrect bloom filter computation causes receipt_root and block bloom divergence.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect bloom causes receipt_root mismatch and block bloom mismatch, both consensus-critical checks.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Must demonstrate bloom filter mismatch causing consensus divergence.

properties.text: Block hash list must return at most 256 hashes in ascending block number order. get_last_256_block_hashes (SG-002) must return hashes ordered by increasing block number, with length <= 256.

reachability.notes: Internal function for building the BLOCKHASH opcode environment. Not directly reachable by external attackers.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect block hash list affects BLOCKHASH opcode behavior but is not directly exploitable.

properties.text: Only the most recent block hash is computed via keccak256; earlier hashes use parent_hash from successors. get_last_256_block_hashes (SG-002) must compute keccak256 only for the most recent block header.

reachability.notes: Performance invariant for hash computation. Incorrect implementation would produce the same result but slower.

properties.severity_justification: Performance optimization concern, not a security issue.

properties.text: apply_fork must be an identity transformation for Osaka. apply_fork (SG-001) must return the state unchanged (no irregular state transition at fork boundary).

reachability.notes: Fork application is internal and not reachable by external attackers. Any bug would cause state divergence at the fork block.

properties.severity_justification: An incorrect fork transition would cause state divergence at the fork block but is a one-time event.

properties.text: All exception classes must inherit from InvalidTransaction. Every validation error raised by transaction_validation_dispatch (SG-004 of exceptions.py) must be a subclass of InvalidTransaction.

reachability.notes: Exception hierarchy is an implementation concern. If an exception does not inherit InvalidTransaction, error handling code may miss it.

properties.severity_justification: Type hierarchy bug could cause incorrect error handling but does not directly compromise security.

properties.text: Each transaction validation check must be mutually exclusive (pass xor fail). In the validation dispatch, every check must either pass (continue to next check) or fail (raise exception), with no ambiguous states.

reachability.notes: Logic correctness invariant. An ambiguous check could allow invalid transactions to pass validation.

properties.severity_justification: Logic correctness concern; ambiguous checks would manifest as other specific validation failures.

properties.text: Every error path in transaction validation must terminate by raising an InvalidTransaction subclass. No validation failure may silently succeed.

reachability.notes: If an error path does not raise an exception, invalid transactions could pass validation silently. An attacker could exploit any missing exception to submit transactions that should be rejected.

properties.severity_justification: A missing exception in any validation error path allows invalid transactions to be accepted and executed.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a transaction that should fail validation but is silently accepted.

properties.text: A transaction is valid if and only if it passes all applicable checks. No transaction may be marked valid while failing any validation check, and no transaction may be rejected while passing all checks.

reachability.notes: Completeness and soundness of the validation dispatch. An attacker exploits any gap between the validation checks and actual transaction validity.

properties.severity_justification: Incomplete validation allows invalid transactions; overly strict validation rejects valid transactions (DoS).
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate either an invalid transaction accepted or a valid transaction rejected by the validation dispatch.

properties.text: Blob data presence must be checked before count validation. blob_transaction_validation (SG-005) must verify blob_versioned_hashes is non-empty before checking count against MAX_BLOB_COUNT.

reachability.notes: If count is checked before presence, a blob transaction with empty hash list might produce unexpected behavior in the count comparison.

properties.severity_justification: Ordering of checks is defensive; both checks would reject empty blob data regardless of order.

properties.text: Blob gas limit must be checked before fee sufficiency. blob_transaction_validation (SG-005) must verify total_blob_gas is within limit before checking max_fee_per_blob_gas >= blob_base_fee.

reachability.notes: Checking fee before gas limit could allow an expensive fee calculation on transactions that would be rejected for exceeding the gas limit.

properties.severity_justification: Primarily a DoS concern for validation ordering, not a correctness issue.

properties.text: Blob validation must fail on the first invalid versioned hash (short-circuit). blob_transaction_validation (SG-005) must stop iterating and raise InvalidBlobVersionedHashError upon finding the first invalid hash.

reachability.notes: Not short-circuiting means processing all hashes even after finding an invalid one. With MAX_BLOB_COUNT bounded, the performance impact is minimal.

properties.severity_justification: Performance optimization; bounded by MAX_BLOB_COUNT so impact is negligible.

properties.text: Transaction exception objects must store fee values as Final/immutable for diagnostic inspection. InsufficientMaxFeePerGasError (SG-003) must preserve both transaction_max_fee_per_gas and block_base_fee_per_gas.

reachability.notes: Exception objects are used internally. If fee values are exposed via JSON-RPC, they could disclose transaction details, but JSON-RPC is out-of-scope.

properties.severity_justification: Data preservation in exceptions is a correctness concern, not a security vulnerability.

properties.text: Integer arithmetic in fee calculations must not overflow in fixed-width implementations. effective_gas_fee = tx.gas * effective_gas_price must not overflow. Client implementations using uint64 or uint256 must handle boundary values correctly.

reachability.notes: An attacker submits transactions with gas parameters near uint256 boundary to trigger overflow in client implementations that use fixed-width integers. The Python spec uses arbitrary-precision integers, but Go/Rust/Java implementations may overflow.

properties.severity_justification: Integer overflow in fee calculations could lead to zero-cost transactions or incorrect state, causing consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate an integer overflow in fee calculation leading to incorrect gas deduction in a specific client implementation.

properties.text: Blocks just under MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE with maximally complex transactions must not cause disproportionate resource consumption. Processing time must be bounded relative to block size.

reachability.notes: An attacker sends blocks that pass the size check but contain transactions designed for maximum CPU/memory consumption during execution. Transaction gas limits (EIP-7825) provide some bounding.

properties.severity_justification: Blocks that pass size validation but cause disproportionate processing waste validator resources. EIP-7825 gas limits provide mitigation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a block under MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE that causes disproportionate processing time relative to normal blocks.

properties.text: Format string values in exception messages must not be vulnerable to injection. Exception classes (SG-001 through SG-003) use formatted strings with user-controlled values. Client implementations must not use these values as format specifiers.

reachability.notes: Python f-strings are safe, but Go/Rust/Java format functions may be vulnerable if user values are used as format specifiers. The attack surface is limited to integer values and type names.

properties.severity_justification: Format string injection is a theoretical risk in non-Python implementations. Attack surface is limited to integer and type name values.

properties.text: TransactionTypeError must store transaction_type as immutable (Final[int]) after construction. SG-001 must ensure the type field cannot be mutated after exception creation.

reachability.notes: Immutability of exception fields is an implementation correctness concern, not directly exploitable.

properties.severity_justification: Field immutability is a code quality concern with no direct security impact.

properties.text: Base fee calculation must use ELASTICITY_MULTIPLIER = 2, setting gas target = gas_limit // 2. calculate_base_fee_per_gas (SG-004) must correctly compute the target from the gas limit.

reachability.notes: If the elasticity multiplier is incorrect, the gas target is miscalculated, causing base fee to converge to wrong equilibrium.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect elasticity multiplier would cause base fee divergence between spec and implementation, leading to consensus failure.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate incorrect base fee calculation due to wrong elasticity multiplier.

properties.text: Gas limit must pass check_gas_limit validation within calculate_base_fee_per_gas. calculate_base_fee_per_gas (SG-004) must call check_gas_limit and reject blocks with invalid gas limit changes.

reachability.notes: If check_gas_limit is not called during base fee calculation, a proposer could set arbitrary gas limits without detection.

properties.severity_justification: Missing gas limit check allows unbounded gas limit changes, affecting fee economics and block capacity.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a block accepted with gas limit changes exceeding the allowed adjustment factor.

properties.text: Base fee maximum change rate must be bounded by parent_base_fee // BASE_FEE_MAX_CHANGE_DENOMINATOR per block. calculate_base_fee_per_gas (SG-004) must not allow base fee to change by more than this amount.

reachability.notes: If the change denominator is incorrectly applied, base fee could change too rapidly, destabilizing fee economics.

properties.severity_justification: Excessive base fee changes affect fee predictability and could cause transactions to fail unexpectedly.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate base fee changing by more than the allowed maximum per block.

properties.text: Block hash lookup returns at most 256 hashes. The BLOCKHASH opcode environment setup via get_last_256_block_hashes (SG-002) must be bounded to prevent unbounded memory allocation.

reachability.notes: Internal bound on historical data. Exceeding 256 would only cause memory waste, not a security issue.

properties.severity_justification: Resource bounding invariant with no direct security impact.
